raw_cv_text:
Hjalmar Fors
+1 (415) 739-8542 • hjalmar.fors@gmail.com • https://hjalmar-fors.dev/research
EDUCATION
Carnegie Mellon University — M.S. in Machine Learning 2019 - 2021
Thesis: 'Efficient Fine-tuning Strategies for Large Language Models in Low-Resource Domains' published at ACL 2021
with 287 citations.
University of Washington — B.S. in Computer Science 2015 - 2019
Cum laude, 3.87 GPA; published undergraduate research on neural architecture search achieving 89% ImageNet
accuracy with 60% fewer FLOPs than baseline.
EXPERIENCE
Anthropic — Research Engineer - Foundation Models June 2023 - Present
Optimized multi-head attention mechanisms reducing model inference latency by 18% while maintaining performance
parity on MMLU and HumanEval benchmarks.
Implemented constitutional AI feedback collection pipeline processing 2.3M preference-based training samples,
improving RLHF convergence time by 35%.
Led experimental design and execution for 7 prompt engineering studies across 12 model variants, generating 3 internal
research publications on in-context learning phenomena.
DeepMind (Alphabet) — Machine Learning Engineer - AI Safety January 2022 - May 2023
Developed interpretability analysis framework using sparse autoencoders to decompose 1024-dimensional activation
spaces, identifying 156 distinct behavioral features in language models.
Designed and executed adversarial robustness evaluation suite for 8 model architectures, discovering critical failure
modes affecting 4.2% of safety-critical prompts.
Collaborated with 4-person team to scale model mechanistic interpretability research from toy models to 7B parameter
transformers, improving intervention success rate from 23% to 68%.
OpenAI — Junior AI Researcher August 2021 - December 2021
Implemented and benchmarked 5 competing approaches to neural scaling law extrapolation, achieving 12% lower
prediction error on held-out 40B model training curves.
Accelerated data preprocessing pipeline for vision-language models by 6x through custom CUDA kernels, reducing per-
epoch compute cost from 8 hours to 80 minutes on A100 clusters.
Contributed novel regularization technique increasing zero-shot transfer performance by 7.3 points on downstream CV
benchmarks across 3 model families.
PROJECTS
Token Prediction Efficiency Study — PyTorch, FSDP, Weights & Biases, NVIDIA A100s March 2024 - Present
Engineering efficiency comparative analysis across 11 different token prediction methods on 13B model scale, targeting
25% latency reduction for serving.
Implemented dynamic batching scheduler improving GPU utilization from 67% to 92% under real-world request
distributions from 15 concurrent users.
Constitutional AI Alignment Benchmark — JAX, Ray Tune, Custom evaluation September 2023 - February 2024
harnesses
Co-authored reproducible benchmark suite evaluating alignment of 8 open-source models against 42 constitutional
principles, cited by 12 downstream papers.
Designed scoring rubric achieving 94% inter-annotator agreement across 5,200 evaluation instances, enabling reliable
comparative analysis of model behaviors.
Mechanistic Interpretability Toolkit — PyTorch, ONNX, FastAPI, Docker June 2022 - August 2023
Developed open-source library enabling circuit analysis on transformer models, achieving 8.5K GitHub stars and
adoption by 6 major AI safety research labs.
Optimized computation graph for feature attribution extraction reducing 48-layer model analysis time from 47 minutes to
6 minutes via vectorized operations.
EXTRACURRICULAR
NeurIPS Conference — Program Committee Member 2023 - Present
Reviewed and provided detailed feedback on 18 papers in foundation models and interpretability tracks, contributing to
acceptance decisions for top 23% of 9,400 submissions.
Mentored 4 junior researchers in paper submission strategy and experimental rigor, resulting in 3 papers achieving
poster presentations.
Berkeley AI Research Lab (BAIR) — Guest Lecturer Fall 2023
Delivered 6-lecture series on neural scaling laws and interpretability techniques to 140 graduate students, receiving
4.7/5.0 teaching evaluation scores.
Designed hands-on PyTorch exercises adopted into curriculum for 3 subsequent semesters across 420+ enrolled
students.
TECHNICAL SKILLS
Languages – Python, C++, MATLAB, Bash
Technologies – PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX, Hugging Face Transformers, ONNX, Weights & Biases, Ray Tune, Triton,
CUDA
Tools – Git, Docker, Kubernetes, Slurm, Weights & Biases, Neptune, Jupyter, MLflow, Hydra
